corrections.text: The article attributes quotes from Aaron Bello-Arufe and Renyu Hu to Phys.org (source 1). The Phys.org snapshot was unavailable (HTTP 403) and could not be verified. The same quotes appear verbatim in the Penn State press release (source 0), which was verified. The quotes themselves are accurate; only the secondary citation is imprecise.

The article reports several paper-specific technical details attributed to the published Astronomical Journal paper (IOP Science, DOI 10.3847/1538-3881/ae4fba): host star type G9V, planetary radius 0.810 ± 0.005 Jupiter radii and mass 0.17 ± 0.02 Jupiter masses, methane Bayes factor approximately 700, carbon-to-hydrogen ratio approximately 13 times solar metallicity, NIRSpec G395M instrument, December 23 2024 transit observation date, 4–5× spectral uncertainty inflation from pointing misalignment, a 3-micron ammonia absorption feature, and transit timing variations consistent with an outer non-transiting planet. The IOP Science page was bot-blocked during review (snapshot redirected to a CAPTCHA page), so these details could not be independently verified from the captured snapshot. They are internally consistent with the press release description and plausible for a published AJ paper but are filed here as unverified paper-level specifics.
